# Review: deepmind/774.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line (`import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`), immediately after the copyright block, separated by one blank line.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd≈ës Problem 774`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/774`.
- [ ] **Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.** The module docstring only has the heading, reference link, and a bibliography entry. It does not include a plain-English summary of the problem (e.g., "Is every proportionately dissociated set the union of finitely many dissociated sets?"). The problem description appears only in the theorem docstring below.
- [x] Optional bibliography entry present: `[AlEr85] Alon, N. and Erd≈ës, P., 1985.` (Note: the entry is incomplete ‚Äî missing paper title and journal/venue, but this is optional.)

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos774 ... end Erdos774`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos774`).
- [x] `open Finset` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `Dissociated` has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `ProportionatelyDissociated` has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] No `noncomputable` needed (both are `Prop`-valued, no classical choice involved).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Types use `UpperCamelCase`: `Dissociated`, `ProportionatelyDissociated`.
- N/A: No functions returning types, no local notation, no `@[category test/API]` (these are "consider" items).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_774` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 11]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references the source paper: `[AlEr85]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks "Is every proportionately dissociated set the union of finitely many dissociated sets?" ‚Äî this is a **yes/no question** ("Is...?"), so **Case 1** applies.

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ‚Üî P`. Correct for a yes/no question.
- [x] `answer(sorry)` used since the problem is open.
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked, so `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` would mean "no".
- [x] Quantifiers (`‚àÄ A : Set ‚Ñï, ...`) appear **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research open` ‚Äî a valid category.
- [x] The `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute (not multiple separate tags).
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed.
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order: `5 11`.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both `5` (Combinatorics) and `11` (Number theory) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjecture uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line format, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. Only the main theorem is stated.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `‚Ñï`, `‚Ñù`, `‚äÜ`, `‚àÄ`, `‚àÉ`, `‚Üî`, `‚âÝ`, `‚â•`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run (manual verification required).
- [ ] Custom linters not run (manual verification required).

---

## Summary

**Status: Minor issue**

The file is well-structured and conforms to nearly all checklist items. The custom definitions (`Dissociated`, `ProportionatelyDissociated`) are cleanly written with good docstrings. The `answer()` usage correctly identifies this as a yes/no question (Case 1). AMS codes are appropriate (combinatorics + number theory).

**Issue found:**

1. **¬ß3 ‚Äî Module docstring missing plain-English description.** The module docstring contains only the heading, reference link, and a bibliography entry. It should include a brief plain-English summary of the problem, e.g., "Is every proportionately dissociated set of natural numbers the union of finitely many dissociated sets?" Currently this description only appears in the theorem docstring.

**Notes on formalization correctness (informational):**

- The `A.Infinite` hypothesis in the theorem is a reasonable addition ‚Äî finite sets trivially decompose into singletons (each being dissociated), so the interesting question concerns infinite sets.
- The formalization `‚àÉ n : ‚Ñï, ‚àÉ D : Fin n ‚Üí Set ‚Ñï, (‚àÄ i, Dissociated (D i)) ‚àß A ‚äÜ ‚ãÉ i, D i` correctly captures "union of finitely many dissociated sets".
- The `Dissociated` definition (distinct finite subsets have distinct sums) and `ProportionatelyDissociated` definition (every finite subset contains a proportionally large dissociated subset) appear mathematically sound.

    42‚Üí

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 774

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/774](https://www.erdosproblems.com/774)

[AlEr85] Alon, N. and Erd≈ës, P., 1985.
-/

open Finset

namespace Erdos774

/-- A set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is *dissociated* if all finite subset sums are distinct:
for any two distinct finite subsets $X \neq Y$ of $A$, $\sum X \neq \sum Y$. -/
def Dissociated (A : Set ‚Ñï) : Prop :=
  ‚àÄ X Y : Finset ‚Ñï, ‚ÜëX ‚äÜ A ‚Üí ‚ÜëY ‚äÜ A ‚Üí X ‚âÝ Y ‚Üí X.sum id ‚âÝ Y.sum id

/-- A set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is *proportionately dissociated* if there exists $c > 0$ such
that every finite subset $B \subseteq A$ contains a dissociated subset of size
$\geq c \cdot |B|$. -/
def ProportionatelyDissociated (A : Set ‚Ñï) : Prop :=
  ‚àÉ c : ‚Ñù, c > 0 ‚àß ‚àÄ B : Finset ‚Ñï, ‚ÜëB ‚äÜ A ‚Üí
    ‚àÉ D : Finset ‚Ñï, D ‚äÜ B ‚àß Dissociated ‚ÜëD ‚àß (D.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â• c * B.card

/--
**Erd≈ës Problem 774** (Alon‚ÄìErd≈ës [AlEr85]):
Is every proportionately dissociated set the union of finitely many
dissociated sets?
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
theorem erdos_774 : answer(sorry) ‚Üî
    ‚àÄ A : Set ‚Ñï, A.Infinite ‚Üí
    ProportionatelyDissociated A ‚Üí
    ‚àÉ n : ‚Ñï, ‚àÉ D : Fin n ‚Üí Set ‚Ñï,
      (‚àÄ i, Dissociated (D i)) ‚àß A ‚äÜ ‚ãÉ i, D i := by
  sorry

end Erdos774

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 774

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/774](https://www.erdosproblems.com/774)

[AlEr85] Alon, N. and Erd≈ës, P., 1985.
-/

open Finset

namespace Erdos774

/-- A set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is *dissociated* if all finite subset sums are distinct:
for any two distinct finite subsets $X \neq Y$ of $A$, $\sum X \neq \sum Y$. -/
def Dissociated (A : Set ‚Ñï) : Prop :=
  ‚àÄ X Y : Finset ‚Ñï, ‚ÜëX ‚äÜ A ‚Üí ‚ÜëY ‚äÜ A ‚Üí X ‚âÝ Y ‚Üí X.sum id ‚âÝ Y.sum id

/-- A set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is *proportionately dissociated* if there exists $c > 0$ such
that every finite subset $B \subseteq A$ contains a dissociated subset of size
$\geq c \cdot |B|$. -/
def ProportionatelyDissociated (A : Set ‚Ñï) : Prop :=
  ‚àÉ c : ‚Ñù, c > 0 ‚àß ‚àÄ B : Finset ‚Ñï, ‚ÜëB ‚äÜ A ‚Üí
    ‚àÉ D : Finset ‚Ñï, D ‚äÜ B ‚àß Dissociated ‚ÜëD ‚àß (D.card : ‚Ñù) ‚â• c * B.card

/--
**Erd≈ës Problem 774** (Alon‚ÄìErd≈ës [AlEr85]):
Is every proportionately dissociated set the union of finitely many
dissociated sets?
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
theorem erdos_774 : answer(sorry) ‚Üî
    ‚àÄ A : Set ‚Ñï, A.Infinite ‚Üí
    ProportionatelyDissociated A ‚Üí
    ‚àÉ n : ‚Ñï, ‚àÉ D : Fin n ‚Üí Set ‚Ñï,
      (‚àÄ i, Dissociated (D i)) ‚àß A ‚äÜ ‚ãÉ i, D i := by
  sorry

end Erdos774
